meta.title: Goods bed cost model v6 — worked numbers, fair-wage community state + investment layer
meta.source_doc: Deep OCR of canvas + Bunnings + INV-1507 invoices + Coastal Fasteners + Notion BK + v6 worked-numbers brief
v6_changes_2026_05_29: Community state now pays a FAIR WAGE: community labour $130/bed (band $100-$160) instead of $0 volunteer. Plastic stays $0 (community-collected, free).
Community DIRECT 140.74 -> 270.74 (band 240.74-300.74). Reframed from 'cheapest/vision' to 'economic PARITY with the factory ($275.74), margin stays community-owned'.

Community margin at $750 = $479.26 (64%); at institutional $801 = $530.26 (66%). Community state-5 ($270.74) is ~$5 BELOW the factory ($275.74); only at the top of the labour band (+$160) is it ~$25 more (the dignified-work premium).

Investment layer slotted in (UNVERIFIED/INFERRED): standalone_site_capex (3-site), volume_ramp_v6, demand_mix, margin_waterfall (brokerage/co-op split), debt_service, sensitivity_v6 (incl. 3-way joint downside + brokerage flex floor).
Confidence: community direct + v6 figures INFERRED (from a verified BOM); per-site capex UNVERIFIED (needs vendor quotes); Centrecorp $801 + BOM VERIFIED.
key_corrections_2026_05_28: INV-1507 $600/bed was WEAVE BED (discontinued) — state_1 removed entirely
Canvas: $93.50/cover verified actual (Centre Canvas 50+110×2 covers in 2026), not $95
Hardware total: $5.24/bed (caps $3.20 + screws $1.04 + bolts $1.00) — was $6.70 estimated
Centre Canvas $14,915 mistag retag pending (ACT-IN → ACT-GD)

canonical_bom._note: Mirrors supplier-quotes.ts. Defy-kit path direct materials = $469.79/bed.

steel_poles.source: DNA Steel Direct (Alice Springs, in Notion)

state_2_defy_kits.label: Defy Kits (buy cut+finished kits, assemble in-house)
state_2_defy_kits._note: Fastest path. Best for ramping while facility being set up.

state_3_defy_panels.label: Defy Panels (buy panels, CNC + assemble)
state_3_defy_panels._note: Worst value — paying for panels we could press ourselves. Only use if press is broken.

state_4_factory.label: Factory (raw shred → press → CNC → assemble in-house)
state_4_factory._note: TARGET state + PARITY COMPARATOR for the community state. $474.26 margin at $750 retail (63%). 4 beds/day × 250 working days = 1,000/yr from one facility. The community state ($270.74) sits ~$5 below this — fair-wage community production is economically on par with the centralised factory.

state_5_community.label: Community (waste plastic free, fair-wage paid work)
state_5_community._note: ECONOMIC PARITY with the factory ($275.74) — not the cheap state. At $270.74 it is ~$5 BELOW the factory; only at the top of the labour band (+$160) is it ~$25 more (the dignified-work premium). $479.26 margin at $750 (64%) / $530.26 at $801 (66%). The margin stays community-owned AND the build is dignified paid work, not free volunteer labour.

first_principles_floor._principle: Musk Idiot Index: what's the absolute minimum a bed could cost if every input was bought at raw-market rate and assembled at industry-low labour rates?

components.source: Coastal Fasteners verified + bulk bolt estimate

first_principles_floor._implication: $340.80/bed of supply-chain markup we could capture in-house at scale. That's the capital-investment case.

idiot_index.markup_pays_for: Shredding + pressing + CNC + finishing + Defy margin. BIGGEST LEVERAGE — moves $304/bed if we shred + press + CNC in-house.
Hot-press + Defy margin. Panel-path is the WORST value — buy raw shred or buy finished kits, not panels.
Cut to length + drill + Alice Springs delivery. Already reasonable.
Cut + hem + grommet + sew + sewist labour. Centre Canvas is competitive against raw 12oz cotton at $62/m self-cut ($130/bed).
At-cost from Coastal Fasteners — already raw rate.
Already competitive — Defy assembly is reasonable vs in-house $40-$80.

counterfactual.source: Retail furniture / contract supply mid-2026 AU market scan

capex_required_to_reach_state_4._capital_ask_basis: GROSS capex to reach Factory state. Net ask ~1,954 (low) / ~111,954 (high) after the 110,046 already invested (facility 100,000 + Carbatec tooling 10,046).

qbe_pitch_inputs._principle: QBE standard funder pitch: Capital ask + unit economics + scaling plan. Sliders should expose these levers.
qbe_pitch_inputs._capital_ask_basis: GROSS capex; net ~1,954 / ~111,954 after the 110,046 already invested.
qbe_pitch_inputs.headline_value_per_dollar_at_target: Each $1,000 of capital invested unlocks ~$X/bed cost reduction, ~Y beds/yr capacity

standalone_site_capex._note: v6: a community/standalone site is a smaller plant than the central factory. Three sites modelled. UNVERIFIED — needs vendor quotes. vs v5 single factory $112-222K → ~1.5-2x for 3 distributed sites.

volume_ramp_v6._note: v6 3-site ramp. Per-site capacity 250/yr is a MODELLING ASSUMPTION (flag — not a verified throughput).

demand_mix._note: v6 demand segmentation. Institutional anchor VERIFIED (Centrecorp INV-0291, 107 beds @ $85,712 = $801/bed). Retail/corp-RAP gift price is the BIGGEST UNKNOWN. Community/humanitarian subsidised.

segments.label: Retail / corporate-RAP gift
segments._flag: biggest unknown — no firm retail/RAP price anchor yet

margin_waterfall._note: v6 per-bed waterfall at institutional $801, COMMUNITY production ($270.74 COGS). Splits the landed margin into ACT brokerage (debt service), a cashflow buffer, and the community co-op margin. The act_brokerage slider MUST FLEX (see sensitivity_v6 joint downside).

debt_service._note: v6 debt-service coverage off the ACT brokerage slice. Base 200/bed brokerage on ~480 revenue beds/yr ≈ $96,000/yr. A thin 60/bed UNDER-covers the $500K matching tranche; the brokerage FLOOR if carrying the full $500K is ~$91/bed (>=$35,000/384).
debt_service.brokerage_floor_basis: >=35000/384 ≈ 91/bed if carrying the full 500K matching tranche

sensitivity_v6._note: v6 ±20% sensitivity dials + two load-bearing insights. (1) The Defy HDPE kit dial has ZERO effect on the community state (free collected plastic decouples it from Defy pricing). (2) The 3-way JOINT DOWNSIDE is the load-bearing CDFI/equity design point.
sensitivity_v6._defy_kit_decoupled_reason: community state uses free community-collected plastic, so its COGS is independent of the Defy HDPE kit price.

joint_downside.implication: Under the joint downside the community co-op margin collapses to ~$14/bed while ACT debt is still serviced. Therefore the act_brokerage slider MUST FLEX DOWN in a downside (step to ~100/bed when margin/bed falls below a floor) or it crushes the community. This flex is the key CDFI/equity design point.

mistags_to_fix._note: Verified ex deep OCR 2026-05-28 — 50 + 110 covers @ $93.50, plus duplicate $10,285 to reconcile

mistags_to_fix.supplier: R M Tanner Triple Axle (Weave Bed era, different project)
Zinus Australia (Basket Bed v1 mattress toppers)

open_questions_for_defy: Volume quote at 100 / 500 / 1,000 / 5,000 beds/yr — what does $344.05/bed kit become?
Build-to-supply: cheaper if ACT sources steel (DNA Steel) + canvas (Centre Canvas) direct and supplies to Defy for kit-assembly only?
In-house assembly payback: at what volume does taking the $55.95/bed assembly in-house pay back the $90K-$200K capex?
Confirm INV-1731 freight (3 × $450 = $1,350) Sydney → Sunshine Coast for 1200kg shred + 20 panels?
